papers.title: Role-Based Task Distribution in YAWL
papers.title_ja: YAWLにおけるロールベースのタスク配分
papers.summary: What happens when workflow tasks are assigned to an entire role rather than a specific user?This video introduces the role-based distribution pattern as a resource allocation mechanism in YAWL. The tutorial explains how role-based distribution makes a task available to all users who belong to a specified role, instead of assigning it to a single predefined individual. Using a simple workflow example involving store clerks and managers, the video demonstrates how a task is offered to multiple users simultaneously and remains visible in their work lists until one user accepts it. Once the task is taken, it is automatically removed from the work lists of the remaining users. The behavior is illustrated through both an animated explanation and a live execution in the YAWL system. Viewers learn how role-based distribution supports flexible task allocation in environments where multiple users are equally qualified to perform a task. The example highlights how this pattern balances flexibilit...

papers.summary: The rapid advancement of deep learning models for visual tasks has led to significant progress in many domains. However, a key challenge remains: ensuring that models can generalize effectively to unseen samples or novel classes, especially in real-world scenarios where training data-target pairs are often limited or unavailable, and test data may exhibit shifts in both data and targets. This thesis addresses the problem of shifts across data and targets, proposing novel methods to improve model generalization and transferability in complex settings. The thesis identifies the different shift scenarios for visual tasks and presents their problem forms. The research first relaxes the static target distribution assumption in Multi-Domain Generalization (mDG) tasks, introducing the General Multi-Domain Generalization (GMDG) objective to improve generalization under varying data shifts. However, their maximum antenna gain is limited by their effective aperture or physical size. While the issue of textual tension has been widely used, and abused, in the context of religious polemics, it is virtually ignored in the practice of interreligious dialogue. One reason for the absence of effective Christian-Muslim dialogue on textual tension is the absence of a clear hermeneutical approach that facilitates and promotes mutual understanding between the two religious traditions. The problem of textual tension can be addressed if the strengths of, and opportunities presented by, interreligious dialogue and comparative ethics are brought together. papers.summary: Soviet cinema has always been a central tool for propaganda and the construction of socialism, as Vladimir Lenin emphasized when he called it ''the most important of the arts.'' In the decades following the October Revolution of 1917, this cinema glorified the ideals of the Communist Party, fulfilling the demands of socialist realism and serving the goals of the state. The Stalinist years reinforced this role, imposing strict censorship and artistic homogenization. However, Stalin's death in 1953 marked the beginning of a cultural transformation, notably driven by the Khrushchevite Thaw. This thesis explores the dynamics of Soviet cinema between Stalin's death and perestroika, highlighting the interaction between Soviet cinema and international influences. The Thaw allowed for greater thematic and stylistic freedom, fostering an artistic dialogue with the West. papers.summary_ja: 本論文は、スターリン死後からペレストロイカ期までのソビエト映画を、文化的移転の観点から分析し、プロパガンダ機能と世界との相互作用を検討する。レーニンが「最も重要な芸術」と称した映画がいかに社会主義構築の理想を体現していたかを追跡している。

papers.title: AI and the Gothic
papers.title_ja: AIとゴシック文学
papers.summary: For as long as technology has existed, there have been those who are afraid of its implications and wish to roll back its progress. One way in which this has traditionally been effected is through the use of gothic tropes to render new technologies as frightful or vampiric upon either labour or life. In this chapter, I explore the ways that gothic aspects are applied to the new breed of large language models, broadly dubbed AI. Taking a base history of artificial life and its intersection with gothic literatures, such as Frankenstein, but also more contemporary touchstones, this chapter aims to understand how gothic rhetorics influence societal uptake and acceptance or rejection of new technological inventions. This chapter will naturally also explore the unnaturalness of supposedly "playing God" and creating artificial life, a trope very much rooted in gothic traditions.
papers.summary_ja: 本論文は、ゴシック的トロープを用いて新しい技術への恐怖を表現する文学的伝統を分析し、AIへの不安がいかに歴史的なテクノロジーフォビアの延長として表現されるかを検討する。
papers.novelty_reason: ゴシック文学理論とAI時代の文化的不安を結合させることで、テクノロジーと文化の関係性に新たな枠組みを提示している。

Il convegno esaminerà i diversi modi in cui immagini, strumenti e dispositivi sono stati utilizzati per misurare il cosmo. Dai primi orologi astronomici e globi celesti alle moderne simulazioni digitali, ogni tipo di misurazione rivela sia le possibilità che i limiti dei nostri tentativi di comprendere l’universo.Avvalendosi delle metodologie delle scienze umane e delle scienze naturali, il convegno intende ridefinire il concetto stesso di scaling. papers.title: The Archive at the End of the World: Speculative Preservation and the Politics of Loss
papers.title_ja: 世界の終わりのアーカイブ：投機的保存と喪失の政治学
papers.summary: This chapter proposes the possibility that future textual ecologies will inherit an archive marked not by abundance, as is often supposed, but by profound loss, whether from climate catastrophe, economic disaster, or digital decay. How might textual ecologies prepare for such an archive? Drawing on theories of dark archives, disaster librarianship, and “post-apocalyptic computing,” this chapter develops a speculative preservation framework that treats redundancy, community mirroring, and federated metadata as anticipatory responses to (textual) ecological instability. Rather than assuming infinite reproducibility and perpetual abundance, this chapter centres loss as a constitutive horizon for future textual cultures, asking what new forms of reading, ethics, and governance emerge when preservation is no longer guaranteed.

papers.summary: Highly skilled migration is a complex phenomenon which despite its comparatively small volume continues to receive interest from a variety of disciplines, all seeking to understand what drives individuals to migrate, and where to. papers.title: Agta hunter-gatherer oral microbiomes are shaped by contact network structure

papers.authors: Federico Musciotto, Begoña Dobón, Michael Greenacre, Álex Mira, Nikhil Chaudhary, Guz Deniz Salali, Pascale Gerbault, Rodoplh Schlaepfer, Leonora H. Astete, Marilyn Ngales
papers.summary: Here we investigate the effects of extensive sociality and mobility on the oral microbiome of 138 Agta hunter-gatherers from the Philippines. Our comparisons of microbiome composition showed that the Agta are more similar to Central African Bayaka hunter-gatherers than to neighbouring farmers. We also defined the Agta social microbiome as a set of 137 oral bacteria (only 7% of 1980 amplicon sequence variants) significantly influenced by social contact (quantified through wireless sensors of short-range interactions). We show that large interaction networks including strong links between close kin, spouses, and even unrelated friends, can significantly predict bacterial transmission networks across Agta camps. Finally, we show that more central individuals to social networks are also bacterial supersharers. We conclude that hunter-gatherer social microbiomes are predominantly pathogenic and were shaped by evolutionary tradeoffs between extensive sociality and disease spread.

papers.summary_ja: 指導下第二言語習得（ISLA）における処理の深さ（DoP）が、特に口頭活動を通じた英語文法習得に与える影響を検証する。認知的処理メカニズムと文法習得の関連性を実証的に明らかにしている。

papers.title: Bridging the Gap in Reading Skills: The Role of Home Literacy Environment and Parental Expectations in Chinese Rural-to- Urban Migrant Children
papers.title_ja: 読み書き能力の格差を埋める：中国農村から都市への移民児童における家庭の識字環境と親の期待の役割
papers.summary: Background Research on the Home Literacy Environment (HLE) in China has predominantly centreed on disparities between urban residents (UR) and rural-to-urban migrants (RUM), yielding substantial insights while leaving critical gaps unaddressed. Although existing literature has identified socioeconomic status (SES) as a key determinant of HLE disparities, it has frequently overlooked pivotal factors such as the duration of urban residency and parental expectations (PE) regarding children’s reading proficiency. These variables are integral to developing a holistic understanding of HLE dynamics within RUM families. Furthermore, while the adverse effects of such disparities on low-level reading skills (LLRS) among migrant children are well-established, potential variations in high-level reading skills (HLRS) remain underexamined. Reading constitutes a multifaceted cognitive process involving textual meaning construction. Grounded in theoretical frameworks such as the he triangle model and ...
papers.summary_ja: 中国の農村から都市への移民児童の読み書き能力格差に対して、家庭の識字環境（HLE）と親の期待が果たす役割を検証する。従来のHLE研究が見落としてきた移民児童特有の要因を明らかにしている。

papers.title: The nature and tensions of environmental management control in local authorities
papers.title_ja: 地方自治体における環境管理統制の性質と緊張関係
papers.authors: Christophe Maurel, Christophe Favoreu, London Proofreaders
papers.summary: This exploratory research aims to characterize, on the one hand, the concept of environmental management control (EMC) through the practices that underpin it, and, on the other hand, the tensions associated with it and the implementation of these practices in public organizations. Although EMC is attracting growing interest within the public sector, it seems to be facing technical and governance difficulties. A grid for analyzing management tools and the theory of organizational paradoxes is used to address the issue of characterizing EMC practices and the associated tensions in the local public sector. Various municipalities were mobilized to explore and characterize their first experiences of implementing EMC. Our results enable us to characterize both the tools used and the tensions generated between the objectives pursued in terms of resource management, dialogue with the socio-economic environment, and behavioral orientation.
papers.summary_ja: 公的組織における環境管理統制（EMC）の概念を実践的に特徴づけるとともに、その実装に伴う緊張関係を探索的に検証する。NPMの制約下での環境管理の課題を明らかにしている。
papers.novelty_reason: 環境管理統制は既知概念だが、公的組織での実装プロセスにおける緊張関係の分析は新しい分析視点を提供している。

papers.title: The Role of Social Media Influencers in Promoting Therapeutic Compliance: Implications for Public Health Policy
papers.title_ja: 治療的遵守促進におけるソーシャルメディアインフルエンサーの役割：公衆衛生政策への含意

papers.summary: Treatment adherence (hereafter, adherence) is critical to achieving positive therapeutic outcomes, whereas non-adherence remains a major public health challenge that hinders recovery and increases healthcare expenditures. Yet the mechanisms through which engagement with SMIs promotes adherence are not well understood. Drawing on the source attractiveness model, this study posits that influencer characteristics – specifically social attractiveness and perceived credibility – shape evaluations of health messages and, in turn, persuade followers toward adherence. We examine the relationship between SMI social attractiveness and adherence, test the mediating roles of perceived authenticity and perceived expertise, and explore the moderating role of SMI message type. Using survey data from 475 participants, we find that SM...
papers.summary_ja: 治療遵守が困難な現状において、ソーシャルメディアインフルエンサーが治療コンプライアンス向上に果たしうる役割を検証する。情報消費の新しい経路としてのソーシャルメディアを公衆衛生に活用する可能性を提示している。
papers.novelty_reason: ソーシャルメディアインフルエンサーを治療遵守の促進者として検証する創新的アプローチで、デジタル時代の公衆衛生政策に新たな次元を付与している。

papers.title: A semi-autonomous public organization’s legitimation work: the case of the survival of an actor in the disability field
papers.title_ja: 準自律的公的組織の正当性維持活動：障害領域におけるアクターの生存ケース
papers.authors: Alexandre Salvatori, Sarah Richard, François Grima
papers.summary: This qualitative study, based on semi-structured interviews and ethnography, starts from the observation that little research has been done on maintaining the legitimacy of semi-autonomous public organizations in a context constrained by certain principles of New Public Management. These principles question the legitimacy of public organizations as the most relevant actors for meeting user expectations. In this article, we study the case of GHP, a semi-autonomous public organization of the “agency” type based in France, which manages a public fund. Based on neo-institutional theory, the study seeks to understand the involvement of actors in the dynamics of maintaining the internal and external legitimacy that ensures the sustainability of an organization. We show that maintaining legitimacy and its alignment between internal and external sources is achieved in a process involving four stages: challenges, maintenance, diffusion, and consensus. Institutional entrepreneurs within the orga...
papers.summary_ja: NPM原則に制約された環境下で、準自律的公的組織が正当性を維持するメカニズムを半構造化インタビューと民族誌調査により検証する。制度的プレッシャーと組織的存続の関係を明らかにしている。
papers.novelty_reason: 準自律的公的組織の正当性維持という特定化されたテーマは新しい分析対象であり、NPM制約下での実践的応答を明らかにしている。

papers.title: Towards a transformative and territorial leadership: The case of organic farming development in Occitanie
papers.title_ja: 変容的かつ領域的リーダーシップに向けて：オクシタニー地域における有機農業発展のケース
papers.authors: Pierre Triboulet, Charlène Arnaud, Liz Carey-Libbrecht
papers.summary: The aim of this article is to characterize the modalities of transformative leadership in support of local public action. It posits that this transformation is based on both the democratic and the distributed dimensions of leadership, and that it is concretely manifested in the territory concerned. To operationalize the concept of Territorial Transformative Leadership (TTL), it draws on a case study of a French Region, Occitanie, which is leading the political project to transform production methods from conventional to organic farming (OF). Twenty semi-structured interviews were conducted in Occitanie. Our results show the analytical potential of the concept of TTL, which reveals (dis)equilibria between the two dimensions of TTL, and questions both the ecosystem of actors driving public action, and the interdependencies between ecosystem and territory.
papers.summary_ja: 地域的公的活動支援における変容的リーダーシップの様態を特徴づけ、民主的かつ分散的なリーダーシップ次元がいかに領域内で具体化するかを検証する。ローカルガバナンスの新しいリーダーシップ形態を提示している。
papers.novelty_reason: 領域的・変容的リーダーシップの統合概念は新興的であり、有機農業というコンテキストでの地域民主的実装を記述する先駆的研究である。

papers.title: Is the university an enabling environment? A qualitative study examining the impact of university disability policy for staff

papers.summary: This study aims to address the university social responsibility (USR) in France by examining disability policy for staff. The concept of an enabling environment is utilized, grounded in the capabilities approach. The aim is to understand how a French university fosters an enabling environment, on the one hand, to facilitate the disclosure of disability and, on the other hand, to promote participation in the institution’s disability policy. The present study employs a qualitative methodology, encompassing both primary and secondary data collection. A thematic content analysis is then conducted, utilising QDA Miner software. The results demonstrate the processes ranging from mobilizable resources to achievements. Some positive conversion factors are highlighted (e.g., the visibility of disability ambassadors), but also some negative ones (e.g., lack of time). It is evident that capabilities are observed, thereby strengthening the power of staff to take steps towards recognition as a disa...
papers.summary_ja: フランスの大学における職員障害政策が、ケイパビリティアプローチに基づいた実現可能環境をいかに構築しているかを検証する。大学の社会的責任（USR）を障害包摂の視点から具体的に検証している。
papers.novelty_reason: ケイパビリティアプローチと障害包摂の結合は新しく、大学職員を対象とした具体的検証は適用領域として新規性を持つ。

papers.title: The Role of Collective Bricolage in Creating University Governance Structures: The Example of the Galileo Observatory
papers.title_ja: 大学ガバナンス構造構築における集団的ブリコラージュの役割：ガリレオ天文台の事例
papers.summary: This study investigates the concept of “collective bricolage” within the framework of creating a universe science observatory in France. In the face of significant transformations in higher education and research, this article examines how university stakeholders can employ collective bricolage to adapt to institutional mandates for restructuring. Drawing on Lévi-Strauss’s notion of bricolage and the theoretical contributions of Duymedjian and Rüling (2010), this research investigates how this bricolage can be implemented in the absence of conventional or normative space to support the process, and particularly within a context characterized by conflictual relationships. The case study analyzes the collective bricolage process and its components, demonstrating that the dialectical nature of collective bricolage facilitates the creation of robust governance structures through the negotiation and recombination of resources and rationales from the diverse actors involved.
papers.summary_ja: 高等教育・研究の大規模変動に直面する中で、大学関係者が集団的ブリコラージュ（臨機応変な資源活用）を通じてガバナンス構造を構築する様態を検証する。限定的資源下での創意的な制度構築プロセスを明らかにしている。
papers.novelty_reason: 集団的ブリコラージュをガバナンス構築に適用する視点は新規的であり、高等教育改革期における実践的な組織的適応メカニズムを記述している。

papers.title: China’s Pandemic Biopolitics: Handling HIV/AIDS and COVID-19

Drawing on Michel Foucault’s concept of biopolitics, I examine how the Chinese government’s responses to the pandemics enhanced its control of the population by attempting to manipulate and steer the processes of knowledge production. I critically employ this Foucauldian approach, considering China's unique political landscape, marked by a mix of one-party authoritarianism conjoined with neoliberalism and discuss the significance and challenges of using this theoretical framework in a context different to those of neoliberal modes of governance. The thesis begins with a focus on...

papers.title: Management of healthcare risk waste and non-hazardous healthcare waste: the case of a public hospital in western France
papers.title_ja: 医療リスク廃棄物および非有害医療廃棄物の管理：フランス西部の公立病院事例
papers.authors: Meriam Karaa, Hanane El Bahraoui, Sarah Garidi, Sébastien de Villèle
papers.summary: This article explores the management of hospital waste, specifically healthcare risk waste (HCRW) and non-hazardous healthcare waste (NHCW). Our study focuses on a hospital in western France, considered as a reference facility, and underlines the specific issues it faces. The methodological approach taken is based on a qualitative case study, providing a thorough understanding of waste management, risk analysis and traceability processes. Data were collected via interviews, direct observations and a test audit to assess regulatory compliance. The goal of this research is to analyse current practices and to provide recommendations for HCRW and NHCW management and traceability.
papers.summary_ja: フランス西部の参照病院における医療廃棄物（リスク廃棄物と非有害廃棄物）の管理実態を検証し、当該病院が直面する特定的課題を明らかにする。医療セクターの環境・安全管理の実装課題を分析している。

papers.title: Self-censorship in Saudi cinema: aesthetics and representations
papers.title_ja: サウジアラビア映画における自己検閲：美学と表現
papers.summary: The thesis examines how self-censorship manifests in Saudi cinema and how it defines on-screen representations and aesthetics. Conventional studies on film censorship have typically adopted a binary framework, positioning filmmakers in opposition to institutional censorship. In contrast, this thesis reconfigures the discourse by treating filmmakers as active agents in the censorship process. Framed by the theoretical work of Michel Foucault and Christian Metz, the study moves beyond traditional definitions of censorship to explore how self-censorship operates at multiple levels: internalized, conforming, and resistant. Internalized self-censorship is an unconscious, ingrained power of discipline, manifesting as automatic practices shaped by the filmmaker’s internalization of societal expectations and a result of functioning within a panoptic environment. Conforming self-censorship is a conscious, deliberate response to societal pressures, where the fear of surveillance or public scruti...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はサウジアラビア映画における自己検閲がいかに画面上の表現と美学を定義するかを検証する。従来の映画検閲研究は映画制作者と制度的検閲を対立させる二項的枠組みを採用してきたが、本研究はこれを超える新たな視点を提示する。
papers.novelty_reason: 非西洋の映画検閲を制度的抑圧ではなく美学的実践として再考する視点は、映画研究に新たな方向性をもたらす。

papers.title: Children's artistic expressions : weaving the threads of cultural diversity
papers.title_ja: 子どもの芸術表現：文化的多様性の糸を織る

The study incorporates a mini-ethnographic research design underwritten by the philosophy of practice-based research. The study aims to identify and describe how children in two differing cultural contexts - the UK and Kuwait - respond to the 'Woven Dialogues' sculpture and express themselves through art and woven materials. 78 children aged between 9 and 11 were recruited from two UK schools and one Kuwaiti school. Data were collected through drawings, weavings...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はベドウィン女性が実践する伝統的織物技術アルサドゥを、機能的工芸および文化的実践として検証する。遊牧共同体の物質的資源提供機能と文化的アイデンティティ表現の両面性を分析する。

papers.title: Intimate Ecologies: Queer Speculations on Pleasure, Blackness and Decolonial Aesthetics

papers.summary_ja: 本論文は黒人共同体と自然および非人間的存在との官能的エロティック関係の重要性と継続性を言語化・可視化する。脱植民地化美学とクィア理論を交差させ、新たな美的政治を提示する。

papers.title: Writing as Spiritual Practice: Beyond Dominant Narratives.
papers.title_ja: 精神的実践としての執筆：支配的ナラティブを超えて
papers.summary: This PhD in Creative and Critical Writing – Writing as Spiritual Practice: Beyond Dominant Narratives – explores addiction as a spiritual and developmental condition shaped by extractive corporate capitalism and considers how memoir can both reflect and resist dominant recovery narratives. Drawing on my lived experience of addiction and recovery, as well as my work as a peer support outreach worker, the project examines writing as a spiritual and political practice capable of unsettling ideological narratives that commodify suffering and reinforce neoliberal ideas of the self. Where Practicing Dying emerges from embodied experience, the critical component situates that experience within broader political, spiritual, and theoretical frames. Rather than seeking to prove the memoir’s insights in academic ...
papers.summary_ja: 本博士論文は創造的かつ批評的な執筆を精神的実践として探究し、搾取的企業資本主義によって形成された依存性を扱う。メモアがいかに支配的な回復ナラティブに抵抗できるかを考察する。

papers.title: The Chaosmos of the Mycological Russian Avant-Garde: Key Nodes of Its Aesthetics The Kandinsky Case: The Visuality of Alchemy and the Search for the Invisible
papers.title_ja: 菌類的ロシア前衛芸術のカオスモス：その美学の主要ノード カンディンスキーの事例：錬金術の視覚性と不可視なものの探求
papers.summary: The theme of occultism and the avant-garde seems to move, as it were, from marginalia to a metanarrative. In his concealed essence Kandinsky appears as a mystico-anarchist, for whom, in particular, the concept of spiritual anarchy proves crucial. papers.summary_ja: 本論文はオカルティズムと前衛芸術の関係をメタナラティブとして再検討し、カンディンスキーを事例に錬金術的思想がいかに視覚言語を形成したかを分析する。神秘主義と急進的政治の交差を追跡する。
papers.novelty_reason: オカルティズムを前衛芸術の中心的美学原理として確立し、菌類学や非西洋思想との関連性を開拓する独創的な理論枠組みである。

papers.title: Turkish Folk Instruments and Voices in Contemporary Music
papers.title_ja: トルコ民族楽器と声：現代音楽における実践
papers.authors: Michael P Ellison, Nihat Argun Cakir, Robert Reigle
papers.summary: Turkish Folk Instruments and Voices in Contemporary Music (TFIVCM) is the first instrumentation resource which covers in depth the folk instruments and voices of Türkiye and Anatolia and their inclusion in contemporary music. It is the second book on the topic of makam instruments in the Routledge Transcultural Music Practices series, the first having concentrated on those of classical makam. Practical, basic information about construction, range, tuning, positions, transposition, character, and playing techniques of folk instruments and voices are provided in written form, along with traditional and contemporary repertoire examples. Crucial to demonstrating the book’s contents are multi-camera-angle video recordings of master Turkish folk instrumentalists filmed in the MIAM recording studio in Istanbul, with captions labelling specific techniques, and in some instances overlaid subtitles (in English).
papers.summary_ja: 本書はトルコおよびアナトリア地域の民族楽器と声の深い資源ガイドであり、これらが現代音楽にいかに組み込まれるかをカバーする初の包括的な著作である。マカム楽器の実践と理論を統合する。
papers.novelty_reason: 民族音楽学と現代音楽制作の統合は実践的な新しさがあるが、理論的な新規性は限定的である。

papers.summary: Psychogeography in the Twenty-First Century: Embodiment and the City offers an updated and comprehensive study of urban practice through the intersections of urbanity, sociology, politics, aesthetics, and the sciences. Building on foundational texts such as Merlin Coverley’s work, this study highlights contemporary methodologies that reflect the diverse backgrounds and embodied experiences of psychogeographers in urban environments. Its examination of recent works of key figures, including Laura Oldfield Ford, Nicola White Mudlark, Priti Pandurangan, and McKenzie Wark, emphasizes their intentional use of digital technologies, online platforms and exhibitions to share détourné artifacts (e.g., writings, photographs, drawings, and maps, as demonstrated at the Fourth World Congress of Psychogeography) as counter-narratives to institutionalized portrayals of the city. In doing so, Psychogeography in the Twenty-First Century also underscores the significance of multimodality and transmedial...
papers.summary_ja: 本書は都市実践を都市性、社会学、政治学、美学および科学の交差点から検証する包括的研究である。Merlin Coverleyの基礎的著作を深化させ、現代の都市経験を再理論化する。
papers.novelty_reason: 心理地理学を多学的に更新する試みは新しいアプローチだが、概念的フレームワーク自体の革新性は中程度である。

papers.title: Picture This: A new sociocultural ‘layered listening framework’ that promotes participation through documentary photography, with young children whose families have migrated to the UK.
papers.title_ja: これを見て：移民家族の子どもたちの参加を促進するドキュメンタリー写真による新しい社会文化的『層状聴取フレームワーク』
papers.summary: High quality, equitable early childhood education and care (ECEC) is significant for the participation of children whose families have migrated to the UK. The rich lived experiences, perspectives and funds of knowledge of newly arrived children are largely invisible and there is an increased risk of social exclusion as a consequence. Drawing upon the findings of a three-year study using child-led photography and photo story books to explore three- to four-year-old children’s funds of knowledge, this chapter offers an original theoretical and pedagogic ‘layered listening framework’ (LLF) that blurs sociocultural theory and visual theories for deep listening with young children. This novel conceptualisation, comprising intuitive and wise photography; ambiguity; photographing democratically and ‘elastic spaces’ suggests an expanded guide for understanding how children as ‘photographer-authors’ co-create diverse perspectives about their lived experiences as pedagogical possibilities. Furth...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はUKに移民した家族の子どもたちの参加を促進するための、ドキュメンタリー写真に基づいた新しい教育フレームワークを提案する。子どもたちの生きられた経験と知識の可視化を目指す。
papers.novelty_reason: 写真方法論と多言語教育学の統合は実践的に有意義だが、理論的枠組みの新規性は限定的である。

papers.title: Mnemonic Conviviality in ‘Post-Cold War’ Britain:Entangled Memories in Urban Space
papers.title_ja: 『冷戦後』ブリテンにおける記憶の親密な共存：都市空間に絡み合う記憶
papers.summary: This article asks how sharing everyday memories in Britain’s superdiverse urban spaces can foster understanding across difference. Drawing on narratives and images produced in 39 arts based photography workshops in West Bromwich and Hyson Green, it develops the concept of mnemonic conviviality: a mode of “living with difference” grounded in the exchange of personal, familial, and collective memories. Through a multi sited, multi group methodology – combining photovoice, visual analysis, and ethnographic dialogue – the article demonstrates how ordinary objects, places, and images spark conversations that entangle memories of socialism, post socialism, colonialism and migration. Through these micro narratives, participants forge temporary commonalities while revealing the enduring impact of the Cold War, colonial hierarchies, and racialisation on contemporary British life. The article demonstrates how memory studies can deepen understandings of conviviality in hyperlocal superdiverse spa...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文は超多様な都市空間における日常的記憶の共有がいかに差異を超えた理解を促進するかを検証し、『記憶の親密な共存』という概念を発展させる。
papers.novelty_reason: 社会的多様性と記憶研究を結合する視点は新しいが、既存のメモリースタディーズの枠組み内である。

papers.summary: This chapter explores how decolonial commitments are enacted and contested in institutional spaces, and the possibilities and tensions this can generate. It examines as a case study the ‘Re-imagining Fashion Histories: Tracing Parallel Cosmologies’ module co-taught at Central Saint Martins, London on the MA Fashion Communication course in 2024/25. This module foregrounded the otherwise in fashion historiography to animate and interrogate ‘imagining’ as a practice that constitutes and reinforces coloniality but simultaneously forges decolonial potentialities. ‘Imagining’ acts to define the suppositions, assumptions and representations that have shaped the canon of fashion history, but simultaneously to re-imagine alternative fashion futures beyond an Anglo-Eurocentric and anthropocentric bias. We employ time as a theoretical entry point into discussions concerning power and coloniality. Our contention is that to re-imagine is not to go backwards, but to radically undo, unravel and delin...
papers.summary_ja: 本章はロンドンの中央聖マーティンズ・カレッジで共同教授された『ファッション史の再想像』モジュールを事例として、制度的空間における脱植民地化的実践の可能性と緊張を探究する。
papers.novelty_reason: 美術教育制度内で脱植民地化を実践的に実装する試みは、高等教育の新たな方向性を示唆している。

papers.title: Photographier l'absence. La violence invisible de la disparition forcée en Colombie depuis 1990
papers.title_ja: 不在を写真撮影する：1990年以来のコロンビアにおける強制失踪の見えない暴力
papers.summary: In its final report published in 2022, the Colombian Truth Commission documented 121,768 cases of enforced disappearance in the country between 1985 and 2016, with statistical estimation models suggesting over 210,000 cases. The signing of definitive peace agreements with the country’s oldest guerrilla group (FARC-EP) in 2017 may have marked an official end to the armed conflict, yet the issue of enforced disappearances remains highly relevant: not only because disappearances continue to occur today, but also because the country faces memorial debt toward its thousands of missing persons and their families.Enforced disappearance is commonly described as an “invisible” crime, deprived of possible images. Indeed, this form of violence aims both at ensuring the impunity of its perpetrators and at the radical erasure—both physical and identity-based—of its victims, leaving their relatives in a state of uncertainty and terror. In this sense, it constitutes a form of violence devoid of visib...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文は2022年のコロンビア真実委員会報告書が記録した121,768件の強制失踪事件（1985-2016年）に焦点を当て、写真表現を通じてこの見えない暴力を可視化し、記憶と正義の問題を追求する。
papers.novelty_reason: 強制失踪という見えない暴力を写真の不在性を通じて理論化する独創的なアプローチは、人権美学の新たな文脈を開拓している。

papers.title: Fashion on the Frontier: Photography, Time and Colonial Modernity on the Madeira-Mamoré Railroad, Brazil
papers.title_ja: フロンティアのファッション：ブラジル・マデイラ=マモレ鉄道における写真、時間、植民地的モダニティ
papers.summary: Fashion on the Frontier: Photography, Time and Colonial Modernity on the Madeira-Mamoré Railroad, Brazil (2026) examines the relationship between fashion, photography and the temporal regimes of colonial modernity in the Brazilian Amazon. It explores how land was demarcated with the expansion of the North American frontier in South America, but also how the contours of different ethnic and racialised bodies were delineated and articulated through fashion and via the pseudo-scientific medium of photography. It moves across diverse fields of thought to consider histories and theories of photography and fashion, modernity and coloniality, labour and domesticity, gender and whiteness, whilst deploying innovative and pertinent historical methodologies for thinking about archival hegemonies and silences. By interrogating History as a critical practice in the fashioning of Latin America, her research questions the emphases and erasures that haunt Brazilian fashion histories.
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はブラジル・アマゾンのマデイラ=マモレ鉄道建設に伴う土地拡張の過程において、ファッション、写真、植民地的モダニティの時間体制の関係を検証する。

papers.summary: This paper explores Aphra Behn’s The Rover (1677) as a complex interrogation of gender roles, libertinism, and the commodification of female sexuality in Restoration comedy. Drawing from Thomas Killigrew’s Thomaso, the Wanderer (1664), Behn both inherits and subverts the conventions of the genre, particularly through her treatment of masquerade, gaze, and social identity. Focusing on the figures of Angellica Bianca, Hellena, and Florinda, the study analyses how female characters navigate patriarchal constraints, erotic desire, and the unstable dichotomy between virgin and whore. It also examines Behn’s critique of violent male libertinism, exemplified by the character of Willmore, and her strategic use of theatrical devices such as costuming and misrecognition. The carnival setting, with its temporary suspension of societal norms, serves as a crucial backdrop for exploring the fluidity of gendered performance. Ultimately, the paper argues that The Rover challenges contemporary sexual i...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はAphra Behnの『浮浪者』（1677年）を性別役割、放縦主義、レストレーション喜劇における女性の性の商品化に関する複雑な問題提起として分析し、Thomas Killigrewとの相続と転覆の関係を追跡する。
papers.novelty_reason: 初期近代演劇における性表現を視覚的欲望の観点から分析するアプローチは有効だが、既存の文学史的枠組み内である。

papers.title: Theatre of the Tech-Oppressed: Exploring algorithmic injustice through participatory performances
papers.title_ja: テック・被抑圧者の演劇：参加型パフォーマンスを通じたアルゴリズム的不正義の探究
papers.authors: Babai, Najme, Duvall, Eva, Iivari, Netta, Kinnula, Marianne, Iversen, Ole Sejer; id_orcid 0000-0002-8055-6716
papers.summary: As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes embedded in systems that shape everyday decisions, it is critical to engage young people in making sense of these technologies and their sociotechnical implications. This paper explores how participatory, theater-based methods, particularly Theater of the Oppressed, can foster critical dialogues among youth about algorithmic decision-making, bias, and injustice. Drawing on a workshop with 15–17-year-olds, we analyze forum theater performances that surfaced questions about power, merit, data, and justice in AI systems for decision-making. Through reflective thematic analysis of the performances and spectactor interventions, we identify how participants impersonated algorithmic agents, questioned meritocratic ideals, exposed the role of data in enabling bias, and experimented with hierarchical and imaginative strategies for intervention. Our findings demonstrate the potential of embodied, performative approaches to support computational empowerment...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はAIがいかに日常的決定を形作るシステムに組み込まれているかを踏まえ、参加型演劇的手法、特に被抑圧者の演劇を用いて若者をテクノロジーと社会技術的含意の理解に導く。

papers.title: Russian Historical Anarchism Reconsidered: Crossroads of Modernism, Political Imagination, and Cultural Theory
papers.title_ja: ロシア歴史的アナーキズムの再検討：モダニズム、政治的想像力、文化理論の交差点
papers.summary: This paper offers a comprehensive re-evaluation of Russian historical anarchism, interpreting it not as a marginal ideological strain but as a metaphysical and aesthetic movement, the intensity of which exceeds the constraints of conventional political theory. Tracing its genealogy from Bakunin to the Gordin brothers, and engaging with figures as diverse as Tolstoy, Bely, and Malevich, the study reveals a continuum between ontological rebellion, symbolic sabotage, and the aesthetic production of radical form. Russian anarchism is shown to operate not merely as political refusal but as a cultural and semiotic matrix that undercuts the metaphysics of order and representation. From the radical language of Zaum’ to cosmo-linguistic utopianism, this article delineates anarchism as a modality of epistemic destitution and ontological subversion. Interfacing with Western theories of deconstruction, biopower, and destituent potentiality, the Russian archive reveals anarchism’s deeper vocation: ...
papers.summary_ja: 本論文はロシア歴史的アナーキズムを限定的なイデオロギー系統ではなく、従来の政治理論の制約を超える強度を持つ形而上学的かつ美学的運動として再評価し、Bakuninからの系譜を追跡する。
papers.novelty_reason: アナーキズムを美学的・形而上学的運動として再理論化し、モダニズム研究と政治理論の統合を提示する革新的なアプローチである。
